titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Charles Lamb Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 4110066.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday March 25, 2018 at 21:57

PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[CHARLES LAMB COLLECTION ])//EN" "lamb.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

did.unittitle: Charles Lamb collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Correspondence and writings by or relating to Charles Lamb. The correspondence includes letters to Edward Moxon, Basil Montagu, and William Wordsworth. The writings includes an autograph manuscript of "Elia to his Correspondents," and a sonnet, beginning "They talk of time, and of time's galling yoke...." Accompanying material includes a pamphlet on Lamb by Edward Moxon, no. 703 of The Mirror, and two engraved portraits of Lamb.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Ongoing collection of documents acquired by gift and purchase from various sources. Type of accession (gift or purchase) and date of acquisition is noted in the box-and-folder list. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Charles Lamb Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Charles Lamb Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This finding aid was produced from previously existing cards in the Manuscripts Catalog, or from another inventory. All pertinent bibliographical information has been retained. If the collection also contains unlisted or unsorted material, it has been noted in the box-and-folder list. This finding aid will be updated periodically to account for new materials that may be added to the collection. The date of the most recent update is noted on the title page. For information on material that may have been added since the last update, please consult the Public Service Desk.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This finding aid encompasses accessions of single manuscripts and small groups of manuscripts and other papers, by or pertaining to Charles Lamb, which the library has chosen to group and list as an artificial collection. Materials given by Cornelia Cogswell Sage have been organized into the first series. Materials added to the collection by gift and by purchase from various sources are located in Series II.

did.unittitle: Gift of Cornelia Cogswell Sage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Given in memory of Henry Manning Sage,
Yale 1890, by his wife Cornelia Cogswell
Sage, 1954.

did.unittitle: Correspondence
AL to Charles Cowden Clarke, no place

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Paper watermarked 1819. Bound with
Letters to Mrs. Williams and Southey

did.unittitle: AL to Edward Moxon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Signed 'Peter.' Bound with "On Some
of the Old Actors," and "Elia to his
Correspondents"

did.unittitle: ALS to Robert Southey, no place
2 ALS to Mrs. Williams, Enfield

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Concerning Lamb's adopted daughter
Emma Isola, later Mrs. Edward Moxon,

did.unittitle: Writings
"Elia to his Correspondents," autograph ms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed 'Elia'

did.unittitle: "On Some of the Old Actors," autograph ms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed 'Elia'

did.unittitle: Additional Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Other materials acquired from various
sources

did.unittitle: Correspondence
ALS to Frances Brown, no place

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1970

did.unittitle: ALS to "H." [James Augustus Hessey of Messrs. Taylor and Hessey], London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Offering a letter of James Thomson
for publication. Accompanied by the
galley proof of Thomson's letter with
ms. corrections and a note to Hessey
on verso in the hand of Charles Lamb.
Attached to top of galley sheet is
the introductory note (written in an
unidentified hand) used when
Thomson's letter was printed in the
"London Magazine," November 1824
Purchase, 1950

did.unittitle: ANS to Hessey, of Messrs. Taylor and Hessey, London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1933

did.unittitle: ALS to William Hone, no place
ALS to Thomas Hood, [Enfield]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1956

did.unittitle: AN to the editors of the London Magazine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Mentioning Elia. Removed from his
John Woodvil, London, 1802
Purchase, 1947

did.unittitle: ALS to Basil Montagu, no place

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Lamb writes that William Hazlitt is
"lying in bed as bad as man can be
that is alive"
Purchase, 1954

did.unittitle: ALS to [Charles] Ollier, no place

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Accompanied by a typewritten copy
Purchase, 1946

did.unittitle: AL to Peter George Patmore

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1965

did.unittitle: AL (fragment) to John Howard Payne

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1974

did.unittitle: ALS to Samuel Rogers, Enfield

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Accompanied by a sonnet, "To Mrs. S.
Rogers of the Death of his Brother,"
a manuscript in an unidentified
hand
Gift, 1970

did.unittitle: ALS to John Taylor, no place

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1954

did.unittitle: ALS to William Wordsworth, no place

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Mentions William Harrison Ainsworth,
Samuel Taylor Coleridge, and George
Dyer. Paper watermarked 1823.
Ainsworth arrived in London in 1824.
With case carrying bookplate of
Moncure Biddle Purchase, 1961

did.unittitle: ALS to unidentified recipient, [London]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Letter begins, "Dear H..."
Gift, 1970

did.unittitle: ALS from Charles Ryle to Francis John Field [E.I. House]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Telling him of the death of Charles
Lamb
Gift, 1941

did.unittitle: Writings
Lamb, Charles. "In tabulam eximii pictoris B. Haydoni, in quâ Solymoei adveniente Domino," holograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1941

did.unittitle: Lamb, Charles. Original manuscripts. "In tabulam eximii pictoris B. Haydoni, in quâ Solymoei adveniente Domino," "Translation of the Latin verses on Mr. Haydon's picture" and "The three graves"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Presented to Francis John Field by
Edward Moxon, with the portrait here
placed as a frontispiece, and the
letter from Ryle to Field bound in.
"The three graves" is written on the
verso of "The translation."
The paper is water-marked 1818.
The binding is full brown crushed
levant morocco, gilt and blind tooled,
with doublures and end leaves of green
watered silk, gilt edges.
Gift, 1941

did.unittitle: Lamb, Charles. Sonnet, autograph manuscript, signed with initials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Sonnet begins: "They talk of time,
and of time's galling yoke..." On
verso, in pencil: "Endorsed by D. C.
Higg." Accompanied by a note from
Dodd, Mead & Co. concerning the
authenticity of the manuscript.
Gift, 1970

did.unittitle: Lamb, Charles. "The three graves," holograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1941

did.unittitle: Lamb, Charles. "Translation of the Latin verses on Mr. Haydon's picture" holograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1941

did.unittitle: Moxon, Edward. "Charles Lamb," autograph manuscript signed, with initials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1965

did.unittitle: Papers from Original Manuscripts Vol.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Full heading: Papers from the Pocket
of the Original Manuscripts Volume

did.unittitle: Photostat of letter from Charles Ryle to Francis John Field

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Original is in box 4, folder 25
Gift, 1941

did.unittitle: Two engraved portraits of Charles Lamb

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
In a paper wrapper addressed to "F.
Field, Esq. India House" and
inscribed in the upper left corner,
"Portrait with autograph verses --
Charles Lamb", and in the lower left
corner, "With Mr. Moxon's
Compliments."
Gift, 1941

did.unittitle: Charles Lamb , printed pamphlet signed E. M. [Edward Moxon]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1941

did.unittitle: The Mirror no. 703

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1941

did.unittitle: Pages 135-136 from an unidentified publication, containing a tribute to Lamb

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1941

did.unittitle: Oversize
AL to Charles Cowden Clarke, no place (from Series I)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Paper watermarked 1819. Bound with
Letters to Mrs. Williams and Southey

did.unittitle: AL to Edward Moxon (from Series I)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Signed 'Peter.' Bound with "On Some
of the Old Actors," and "Elia to his
Correspondents"

did.unittitle: ALS to Frances Brown, no place (from Series II)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1970

did.unittitle: ALS to "H." [James Augustus Hessey of Messrs. Taylor and Hessey], London (from Series II)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Offering a letter of James Thomson
for publication. Accompanied by the
galley proof of Thomson's letter with
ms. corrections and a note to Hessey
on verso in the hand of Charles Lamb.
Attached to top of galley sheet is
the introductory note (written in an
unidentified hand) used when
Thomson's letter was printed in the
"London Magazine," November 1824

did.unittitle: Lamb, Charles. Original manuscripts. "In tabulam eximii pictoris B. Haydoni, in quâ Solymoei adveniente Domino," "Translation of the Latin verses on Mr. Haydon's picture" and "The three graves"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Presented to Francis John Field by
Edward Moxon, with the portrait
here placed as a frontispiece, and
the letter from Ryle to Field bound
in. "The three graves" is written
on the verso of "The translation."
The paper is water-marked 1818.
The binding is full brown crushed
levant morocco, gilt and blind
tooled, with doublures and end
leaves of green watered silk, gilt
edges.
Gift, 1941
